task_0_1_symmetric_txt_records_ktor.deviations_from_plan: API_VERSION hardcoded to 1.0.1 (vs plan's 1.0.0); BuildConfig wiring deferred per follow-up TODO in proxy/CLAUDE.md.

task_1_1_directory_tree_and_gomod.go_mod_replace_directives: 14 of 14 (auth, cache, challenges, concurrency, config, containers, database, discovery, http3, mdns, middleware, observability, ratelimiter, recovery, security)
deviations_from_plan: go.mod uses go 1.25.0 instead of plan's 1.24 (Go toolchain bump; non-breaking)
Adds digital.vasic.concurrency to replace block (introduced post-plan during Phase implementation)
Uses tool {} block for migrate + oapi-codegen instead of separate require + binstall (acceptable variant)

ci.sh has more steps than plan's skeleton (steps 1-3) because Phases 2/13 already extended it; this is expected per the plan's own note 'Steps are added in the phase that produces the corresponding code'
Adds --strict flag and per-tool LAVA_CI_SKIP_* env vars; documented variant

task_1_4_internal_version_package.Name_format: strict semver (currently 2.0.7 — bumped post-Phase-1 by SP-2 Phase 14)
deviations_from_plan: TestNameAndCodeMatchInitialRelease was REMOVED post-Phase-1 (commented rationale in version_test.go: it was a test-re-asserts-the-constant anti-pattern that would rot at every release tag). The two surviving tests preserve the format invariants which are the real load-bearing properties.
Name/Code values diverged from plan's '2.0.0/2000' to '2.0.7/2007' as expected — scripts/tag.sh rewrites these on each release per Phase 12.

acceptance_gates.lava_api_go_go_test: GREEN (all internal/* packages PASS, no test files in internal/gen/{client,server} which is by design)

deviations_summary: All Phase 0 + Phase 1 tasks PASS. Variances from plan are minor (Go toolchain version bump, additional submodule, removed self-asserting test, extended ci.sh as later phases prescribed). No structural deviations; no missing artifacts. Per Adaptation C of the audit instructions, the variances are 'acceptable variants'.
outcome: PASS — no follow-up work required for Phase 0 or Phase 1; SP-2 audit clean as of HEAD.
